lessons: WRONG fix: the broker-fee table said 'One month + VAT, paid by tenant' and 'Who pays: usually the tenant'. That is the pre-2017 market habit, not the law. Section 25ט of the Rental and Loan Law (Fair Rental Law) makes it NON-WAIVABLE that a residential tenant cannot be charged a fee for a broker who acted on the landlord's behalf; a tenant who independently hires a broker pays (half if both sides used the same broker). Rewrote the Who-pays cell in EN + HE. This was a long-standing missed law, not a 2026 change.
Freshness: the Q3-2025 national average (NIS 4,952) is accurate but early-2026 supply tightened and new-lease asking rents rose, so added a 'treat as a floor' note in both languages.

VERIFIED OK (no change): listing sites all live (Yad2 still dominant, Madlan, Homeless, WinWin, Facebook), VAT 18%, deposit cap = lower of 3 months or one-third of lease, repair windows (~30d/~3d), habitability standard (dira re'uya), mamad post-1992, 2024 broker ethics regs (eff. 9.3.2025) incl. mandatory written heskem-tivuch, Hebrew glossary terms, arnona municipality-specific + oleh discount up to 90% / first 100sqm / 12-of-24 months, small-claims ceiling NIS 39,900 (eff. 1.1.2026).

CARRY (WATCH, not enacted): a Knesset bill to fully ban imposing broker fees on tenants and an amendment to the Brokers Law adding disciplinary liability are advancing but not yet law. Revisit next cycle.

CRITICAL fix (expert-found, pre-existing): the skill said a bank guarantee 'typically equals 3-6 months rent', which exceeds the Section 25י statutory cap. A bank guarantee is a tenant-monetary-outlay security and is capped at the LOWER of 3 months or 1/3 of the lease (~3 months for a 12-month lease). Capped the figure in EN+HE. Also scoped the cap correctly: it applies to bank-guarantee/cash, NOT to a regular security check or promissory note (shtar chov), which may be set higher. Added a Section 25י evidence claim.
MINOR fix: 'only half if both sides used the same broker' was presented as a rule; reframed as a negotiated practice, not a statutory entitlement.
Stripped pre-existing em-dashes from SKILL.md + SKILL_HE.md (em-dash ban applies to touched files).
